features.name: Right Atrium
features.description: Receives deoxygenated blood from the body via the superior and inferior vena cava.

features.name: Left Atrium
features.description: Receives oxygenated blood from the lungs via four pulmonary veins.

features.name: Right Ventricle
features.description: Pumps deoxygenated blood to the lungs through the pulmonary artery.

features.name: Left Ventricle
features.description: The most muscular chamber, pumps oxygenated blood to the entire body through the aorta.

features.name: Aorta
features.description: The largest artery in the body, carries oxygenated blood from the left ventricle to systemic circulation.

features.name: Pulmonary Artery
features.description: Carries deoxygenated blood from the right ventricle to the lungs for gas exchange.

features.name: Tricuspid Valve
features.description: Three-leaflet valve between the right atrium and right ventricle, prevents backflow.

features.name: Mitral Valve
features.description: Bicuspid valve between the left atrium and left ventricle, also called the bicuspid valve.

features.name: Superior Vena Cava
features.description: Large vein that returns deoxygenated blood from the upper body to the right atrium.

features.name: Inferior Vena Cava
features.description: Largest vein in the body, returns blood from the lower body to the right atrium.

features.name: Interventricular Septum
features.description: Muscular wall separating the left and right ventricles.

features.name: Coronary Arteries
features.description: Supply oxygenated blood to the heart muscle itself. Blockage causes heart attacks.

features.name: Frontal Lobe
features.description: Controls executive functions, decision making, personality, and voluntary movement.

features.name: Parietal Lobe
features.description: Processes sensory information including touch, temperature, and spatial awareness.

features.name: Temporal Lobe
features.description: Processes auditory information, memory formation, and language comprehension.

features.name: Occipital Lobe
features.description: Primary visual processing center, interprets signals from the eyes.

features.name: Cerebellum
features.description: Coordinates balance, posture, motor learning, and fine movement control.

features.name: Brain Stem
features.description: Controls vital functions: breathing, heart rate, blood pressure, and consciousness.

features.name: Corpus Callosum
features.description: Band of nerve fibers connecting the left and right cerebral hemispheres.

features.name: Thalamus
features.description: Relay station that routes sensory and motor signals to the cerebral cortex.

features.name: Hypothalamus
features.description: Regulates body temperature, hunger, thirst, sleep, and hormonal balance.

features.name: Hippocampus
features.description: Critical for forming new memories and spatial navigation.

features.name: Amygdala
features.description: Processes emotions, especially fear and anxiety responses.

features.name: Motor Cortex
features.description: Located in the frontal lobe, directly controls voluntary muscle movements.

features.name: Right Upper Lobe
features.description: Uppermost section of the right lung, separated by the horizontal fissure.

features.name: Right Middle Lobe
features.description: Unique to the right lung, located between horizontal and oblique fissures.

features.name: Right Lower Lobe
features.description: Largest lobe of the right lung, extends to the diaphragm.

features.name: Left Upper Lobe
features.description: Contains the cardiac notch that accommodates the heart.

features.name: Left Lower Lobe
features.description: Separated from the upper lobe by the oblique fissure.

features.name: Trachea
features.description: Windpipe connecting the larynx to the bronchi, reinforced with C-shaped cartilage rings.

features.name: Right Main Bronchus
features.description: Wider, shorter, and more vertical than the left, making it more susceptible to aspiration.

features.name: Left Main Bronchus
features.description: Narrower and longer, angles more horizontally than the right bronchus.

features.name: Alveoli
features.description: Tiny air sacs (~480 million) where gas exchange occurs with surrounding capillaries.

features.name: Pleural Membrane
features.description: Double-layered membrane surrounding each lung, contains lubricating fluid.

features.name: Diaphragm
features.description: Primary muscle of respiration, contracts to expand the thoracic cavity.

features.name: Hilum
features.description: Region where bronchi, blood vessels, and nerves enter each lung.

features.name: Renal Cortex
features.description: Outer region containing glomeruli and proximal/distal convoluted tubules for blood filtration.

features.name: Renal Medulla
features.description: Contains renal pyramids with loops of Henle and collecting ducts for urine concentration.

features.name: Renal Pelvis
features.description: Funnel-shaped structure that collects urine from calyces and channels it to the ureter.

features.name: Hilum
features.description: Concave medial border where renal artery, vein, ureter, and nerves enter/exit.

features.name: Renal Artery
features.description: Branches from the abdominal aorta, delivers ~20% of cardiac output to each kidney.

features.name: Renal Vein
features.description: Returns filtered blood from the kidney to the inferior vena cava.

features.name: Ureter
features.description: Muscular tube that transports urine from the renal pelvis to the urinary bladder.

features.name: Nephron
features.description: Functional unit of the kidney (~1 million per kidney), filters blood and produces urine.

features.name: Renal Capsule
features.description: Tough fibrous layer that protects the kidney from trauma and infection.

features.name: Renal Calyces
features.description: Cup-shaped structures that collect urine draining from the renal pyramids.

features.name: Right Lobe
features.description: The larger lobe (~6x larger than left), performs most metabolic and detoxification functions.

features.name: Left Lobe
features.description: Smaller, thinner lobe that extends across the midline towards the spleen.

features.name: Hepatic Portal Vein
features.description: Carries nutrient-rich blood from the digestive organs for processing.

features.name: Gallbladder
features.description: Pear-shaped organ that stores and concentrates bile produced by the liver.

features.name: Hepatic Artery
features.description: Supplies oxygenated blood to the liver from the celiac trunk.

features.name: Hepatic Veins
features.description: Three major veins that drain processed blood into the inferior vena cava.

features.name: Caudate Lobe
features.description: Small lobe on the posterior surface, wraps around the inferior vena cava.

features.name: Quadrate Lobe
features.description: Small lobe on the inferior surface, between the gallbladder and round ligament.

features.name: Common Bile Duct
features.description: Carries bile from the liver and gallbladder to the duodenum for fat digestion.

features.name: Falciform Ligament
features.description: Separates the right and left lobes, attaches the liver to the anterior abdominal wall.

features.name: Fundus
features.description: Upper dome-shaped portion that often accumulates gas after eating.

features.name: Body (Corpus)
features.description: Main central region containing gastric glands that produce HCl and pepsinogen.

features.name: Pylorus
features.description: Sphincter-controlled passage that regulates food entry into the duodenum.

features.name: Cardia
features.description: Junction where the esophagus meets the stomach, has a sphincter to prevent reflux.

features.name: Pyloric Antrum
features.description: Lower portion that mixes food with gastric juices before release through pylorus.

features.name: Greater Curvature
features.description: Long convex lateral border of the stomach, attachment for the greater omentum.

features.name: Lesser Curvature
features.description: Short concave medial border, attachment for the lesser omentum.

features.name: Rugae
features.description: Internal mucosal folds that allow the stomach to expand when filled with food.

features.name: Gastric Glands
features.description: Produce gastric acid (pH 1.5-3.5), pepsinogen, mucus, and intrinsic factor.

features.name: Lower Esophagus
features.description: Muscular tube that connects the pharynx to the stomach via peristalsis.

features.name: Cornea
features.description: Transparent avascular dome that performs ~70% of the eye's total refractive power.

features.name: Iris
features.description: Colored muscular diaphragm that controls pupil size and regulates light entry.

features.name: Pupil
features.description: Central aperture in the iris, dilates in dim light and constricts in bright light.

features.name: Crystalline Lens
features.description: Biconvex transparent structure that focuses light onto the retina via accommodation.

features.name: Retina
features.description: Light-sensitive neural layer with ~120M rods and ~6M cones converting light to nerve signals.

features.name: Optic Nerve
features.description: Bundle of ~1.2 million nerve fibers transmitting visual information to the brain.

features.name: Sclera
features.description: Tough white outer coat that maintains eye shape and protects internal structures.

features.name: Vitreous Body
features.description: Gel-like substance filling the posterior chamber, maintains eye shape and optical clarity.

features.name: Ciliary Body
features.description: Produces aqueous humor and changes lens shape for focusing at different distances.

features.name: Macula & Fovea
features.description: Central region of retina responsible for sharp central vision and color perception.

features.name: Conjunctiva
features.description: Thin mucous membrane covering the sclera and inner eyelids, provides lubrication.

features.name: Aqueous Humor
features.description: Clear fluid in the anterior chamber, maintains intraocular pressure and nourishes cornea.

features.name: Pinna (Auricle)
features.description: External ear cartilage that collects and funnels sound waves into the ear canal.

features.name: External Acoustic Meatus
features.description: S-shaped canal (~2.5cm) that channels sound waves to the tympanic membrane.

features.name: Tympanic Membrane
features.description: Thin semitransparent membrane that vibrates in response to sound pressure waves.

features.name: Cochlea
features.description: Spiral-shaped organ with ~15,000 hair cells that converts mechanical vibrations to nerve signals.

features.name: Malleus (Hammer)
features.description: Largest ossicle, attached to the tympanic membrane, receives and transmits vibrations.

features.name: Incus (Anvil)
features.description: Middle ossicle connecting the malleus to the stapes, amplifies sound vibrations.

features.name: Stapes (Stirrup)
features.description: Smallest bone in the body, transmits vibrations to the oval window of the cochlea.

features.name: Vestibular System
features.description: Three semicircular canals that detect rotational movement and maintain balance.

features.name: Eustachian Tube
features.description: Connects middle ear to nasopharynx, equalizes air pressure across the tympanic membrane.

features.name: Auditory Nerve
features.description: Cranial nerve VIII, transmits auditory and vestibular information to the brainstem.

features.name: Oval Window
features.description: Membrane-covered opening connecting the middle ear to the inner ear cochlea.

features.name: Skull (Cranium)
features.description: 22 fused bones protecting the brain and supporting facial structures.

features.name: Vertebral Column
features.description: 33 vertebrae (7 cervical, 12 thoracic, 5 lumbar, 5 sacral, 4 coccygeal) protecting the spinal cord.

features.name: Rib Cage
features.description: 12 pairs of ribs forming a protective cage around the heart and lungs.

features.name: Pelvis
features.description: Basin-shaped structure supporting the upper body and connecting to lower limbs.

features.name: Femur
features.description: Longest and strongest bone, supports body weight during standing and walking.

features.name: Clavicle
features.description: S-shaped collarbone connecting the scapula to the sternum, most commonly fractured bone.

features.name: Scapula
features.description: Triangular flat bone on the posterior thorax, provides attachment for arm muscles.

features.name: Humerus
features.description: Upper arm bone, articulates with scapula at shoulder and radius/ulna at elbow.

features.name: Tibia
features.description: Shinbone, second largest bone, bears most of the weight transmitted to the lower leg.

features.name: Sternum
features.description: Flat bone in the center of the chest, connects to ribs and protects the heart.

features.name: Mandible
features.description: Largest and strongest facial bone, the only movable bone of the skull.

features.name: Patella
features.description: Kneecap, largest sesamoid bone, protects the knee joint and improves leverage.

features.name: Enamel
features.description: Hardest substance in the human body (96% mineral), protects the crown from wear and decay.

features.name: Dentin
features.description: Makes up the bulk of tooth structure, harder than bone but softer than enamel, contains tubules.

features.name: Pulp Chamber
features.description: Central soft tissue containing nerves, blood vessels, and connective tissue that nourish the tooth.

features.name: Root
features.description: Anchors the tooth in the alveolar bone of the jaw, covered by cementum.

features.name: Cementum
features.description: Thin calcified layer covering the root, provides attachment point for periodontal ligament.

features.name: Periodontal Ligament
features.description: Connective tissue fibers that suspend the tooth in its socket, act as shock absorbers.

features.name: Gingiva (Gums)
features.description: Soft mucosal tissue covering the alveolar bone, forms a seal around each tooth.

features.name: Root Canal
features.description: Narrow channel within the root containing pulp tissue extending to the apex.

features.name: Anatomical Crown
features.description: Visible part of the tooth above the gumline, covered by enamel.

features.name: Root Apex
features.description: Tip of the root where blood vessels and nerves enter through the apical foramen.

features.name: Alveolar Bone
features.description: Specialized bone of the jaw that forms the sockets holding each tooth.
